name: CodeRabbit API
what_it_does: AI-powered code review platform that automatically reviews pull requests on GitHub and GitLab, providing line-by-line suggestions, summaries, and security feedback.
use_cases: Automated AI code review on every pull request
Generating PR summaries and change documentation
Enforcing code quality standards via configurable review rules
Security vulnerability detection in code changes
Agents orchestrating code review workflows as part of CI/CD pipelines
not_for: Review of proprietary codebases with strict data residency requirements
Languages or frameworks with very niche or minimal community support
Teams needing fully on-premises AI processing with no cloud calls
best_when: A team wants automated, high-quality AI code review on every PR without manually configuring LLM prompts or review pipelines.
avoid_when: Your organization prohibits sending source code to third-party AI services, or you need a self-hosted-only solution.

auth.notes: GitHub App or GitLab integration via OAuth. API tokens available for configuration management. Scopes are controlled at the GitHub/GitLab App permission level.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for public/open source repositories; unlimited PR reviews
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro at $15/user/month; Enterprise with custom pricing and self-hosted options
pricing.notes: Free tier is genuinely full-featured for open source. Private repo usage requires paid plan.

performance.rate_limits: Review requests tied to PR events; API calls limited by tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses from the REST API are JSON-structured but thin on detail; review event errors surface through GitHub/GitLab webhook payloads rather than a direct API response.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Review triggers are event-driven via GitHub/GitLab webhooks; retriggering via API is possible but may produce duplicate review comments on the same PR.
known_agent_gotchas: No official REST API client SDKs; agents must implement HTTP calls manually
Review latency varies widely (30s to 2min+) depending on PR size; agents should poll or use webhooks rather than expecting a synchronous response
Configuration is per-repository via .coderabbit.yaml; agents modifying repos must manage this file
GitHub App permissions must be explicitly granted per organization before the API is usable
Rate limits on review triggering are not clearly documented and may change without notice

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth via GitHub/GitLab App model provides reasonable permission scoping. SOC2 certified. Source code is transmitted to CodeRabbit's AI backend; review their data handling policy for compliance-sensitive use cases.
